text.title: QA Metrics & SLOs Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `QA Metrics & SLOs` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The methodology fires on a named trigger (event, threshold, or schedule). 'When needed' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit trigger, the artefact either fires constantly or never; cadence becomes unenforceable.

rule.statement: Output shape is fixed (template, schema, or artefact path). Free-form output is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Reusable artefacts require a shape; free-form output is one-off and dies on completion.

rule.statement: Every metric conclusion links to evidence — dashboards, tickets, CI run links, mutation reports. Unevidenced conclusions are demoted to hypotheses.
rule.rationale: Faion has zero coverage of QA-as-a-product metrics. Without evidence anchors, modernization regresses to opinion.

rule.statement: Every instance has a named human owner accountable for next-step actions. Team/channel ownership is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous outputs do not produce behaviour change.

rule.statement: The methodology is reviewed against outcomes on a published cadence (monthly or quarterly). Methodologies producing no measurable change are deprecated.
rule.rationale: Methodologies without an outcome review become folklore.

rule.statement: The published metric set MUST include: escape rate, MTTD-bug, MTTR-bug, flake rate, mutation score (or coverage proxy), eval-coverage SLO (if AI-features in scope), CI lead time. Missing any → INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Subset metrics encourage gaming. The floor set keeps regression visible across the value stream.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
